text:
the child[ren] of the family [until [he] / [she] / [they] shall [respectively] attain the age of 18 or cease [his] / [her] / [their] full-time secondary education] / [for so long as the order for periodical payments at paragraph [para number] below in respect of [him] / [her] / [them] shall subsist].

at [scale of cover (e.g. at the same level of cover as currently provided)].

If the [applicant] / [respondent] [and] / [or] the child[ren] of the family cannot remain part of the existing medical insurance cover, the [respondent] / [applicant] shall pay for separate medical insurance cover with [the same insurance company] / [a reputable United Kingdom insurance company] for:

the [applicant] / [respondent] [until [he] / [she] shall remarry] / [for so long as the order for periodical payments at paragraph [para number] below in the [applicant’s] / [respondent’s] favour shall subsist]; [and] / [or]

the child[ren] of the family [until they shall respectively attain the age of 18 or cease their full-time secondary education] / [for so long as the order for periodical payments at paragraph [para number] below in respect of them shall subsist].

at [specify scale of cover (e.g. at the same level of cover currently provided)].

The [respondent] / [applicant] shall provide the [applicant] / [respondent] with written evidence within 14 days of the date of this order that this medical insurance cover is in place, and shall provide the [applicant] / [respondent] with written evidence within 14 days of the date of renewal each year that this medical insurance cover remains in place.

When the order for periodical payments at paragraph [para number] below in the [applicant’s] / [respondent’s] favour comes to an end such that the [respondent] / [applicant] is no longer obliged to provide medical insurance cover for the [applicant] / [respondent]; [and] / [or]

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 1: Financial Remedies (May 2026)
metadata.title: In the Family Court		Case No: [Case number]
